decision.read: Shrinking the D tile halves the per-CTA active-group accumulator columns, but it doubles the D-block launch/work count and loses MFMA/tile efficiency. The S4096 HSK bf16 source path regresses from about 5.0 ms to 8.5 ms, so the LDS conflict problem cannot be solved by a smaller D tile alone.
decision.next_kernel_direction: Keep block_d=64 and reduce the number or conflict pattern of group_acc read-modify-write operations inside the existing single producer pass. Viable next probes are wave-private/lane-group accumulation before group_acc, generated-source-level LDS layout inspection, or a lower-level HIP/CK/TileLang schedule that accumulates multiple selected rows for a hot group in registers before touching LDS.
